use tonic::{Request, Response, Status};
use tonic::metadata::MetadataMap;

struct RequestContext {
    tenant_id: String,
    trace_id: String,
    user_id: String,
    role: String,
}

impl RequestContext {
    fn from_metadata(md: &MetadataMap) -> Result<Self, Status> {
        // GOOD: parse metadata once into a structured context
        let tenant_id = md.get("x-tenant-id")
            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
            .map(|s| s.to_string())
            .ok_or_else(|| Status::invalid_argument("missing tenant"))?;
        let trace_id = md.get("x-trace-id")
            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
            .map(|s| s.to_string())
            .ok_or_else(|| Status::invalid_argument("missing trace"))?;
        let user_id = md.get("x-user-id")
            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
            .map(|s| s.to_string())
            .ok_or_else(|| Status::invalid_argument("missing user"))?;
        let role = md.get("x-role")
            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
            .map(|s| s.to_string())
            .ok_or_else(|| Status::invalid_argument("missing role"))?;

        Ok(Self { tenant_id, trace_id, user_id, role })
    }
}

struct MyService;

#[tonic::async_trait]
impl MyService {
    async fn process(&self, req: Request<()>) -> Result<Response<()>, Status> {
        let ctx = RequestContext::from_metadata(req.metadata())?;
        Ok(Response::new(()))
    }
}
